title: Rhaphiostylis parvifolia

The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in African cultures, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Rhaphiostylis parvifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Rhaphiostylis parvifolia is a plant species classified under the family Sapindaceae, commonly known as the soapberry family. This family comprises a large number of species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Rhaphiostylis parvifolia is a member of the order Sapindales, a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their simple leaves and fruits that often contain a milky sap.
